texto: You don't feel thirty percent smarter when the stock goes up by thirty percent so when the stock goes down you shouldn't feel thirty percent dumber" Jeff Bezos
explicacao: Orientação direta de Brad Stone sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “You don't feel thirty percent smarter when the stock goes up by thirty percent so when the stock …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, business, common-sense, útil em momentos de inspiracao e common-sense.

frase_original: You don't feel thirty percent smarter when the stock goes up by thirty percent so when the stock goes down you shouldn't feel thirty percent dumber" Jeff Bezos

texto: It was the combination of EC2 and S3 - storage and compute, two primitives linked together - that transformed both AWS and the technology world. Startups no longer needed to spend their venture capital on buying servers and hiring specialized engineers to run them. Infrastructure costs were variable instead of fixed, and they could grow in direct proportion to revenues. It freed companies to experiment, to change their business models with a minimum of pain, and to keep up with the rapidly growing audiences of erupting social networks like Facebook and Twitter.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Brad Stone: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “It was the combination of EC2 and S3 - storage and compute, two primitives linked together - that…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, entreprenuership, innovation pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e termino.

frase_original: It was the combination of EC2 and S3 - storage and compute, two primitives linked together - that transformed both AWS and the technology world. Startups no longer needed to spend their venture capital on buying servers and hiring specialized engineers to run them. Infrastructure costs were variable instead of fixed, and they could grow in direct proportion to revenues. It freed companies to experiment, to change their business models with a minimum of pain, and to keep up with the rapidly growing audiences of erupting social networks like Facebook and Twitter.
